steps.title: Set up a time equation
steps.content: The trip lasted $3$ hours total. Since $x$ is the time on the highway and $y$ is the time on local streets, the times add to the total: $x+y=3$.

steps.title: Set up a distance equation
steps.content: Distance equals rate times time. On the highway, the shuttle covered $60x$ miles; on local streets, it covered $30y$ miles. Together these equal the $150$ mile trip: $60x+30y=150$.

steps.title: Match the correct system
steps.content:
The system is $60x+30y=150$ and $x+y=3$.
  • A) $60x+30y=3$, $x+y=150$ swaps the totals: the distances equal $3$ and times equal $150$, which is impossible.
  • B) $60x+30y=150$, $x+y=3$ matches the distance and time equations derived above.
  • C) $30x+60y=3$, $x+y=150$ both swaps totals and assigns $30$ mph to the highway time $x$ instead of $60$ mph.
  • D) $30x+60y=150$, $x+y=3$ has the right totals but pairs the $30$ mph local rate with the highway time $x$ and the $60$ mph highway rate with the local time $y$. That confirms B is correct.
